concept: Drum / Percussion Instrument / To Beat Rhythm
description: A percussion instrument with membrane stretched over frame; to beat rhythmically. 'Kuma' names what speaks in rhythm—the hollow voice that calls community together.
sound_symbolism: The word strikes hollow. 'Ku' hits with resonant impact, membrane responding. Then 'ma' grounds in deep vibration, rhythm felt in body.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun and verb: 'sulai kuma wile.' (The musician plays the drum.) As verb: 'mia kuma' (I drum). Instrument of communication, ceremony ('thorea'), and dance ('rotiku').

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Rhythm as heartbeat—the drum echoes the body's pulse, grounding awareness in present moment.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Drums spoke across distance—calling, celebrating, warning, the voice that travels.
